blocks.statement: I can preview the Car Project parts kit, identify which UNIT each part traces to, and predict the motor's CURRENT and POWER using V = IR and P = VI — BEFORE I build.

blocks.whatWeKnow: FINAL REFRAMING — the asteroid arc is closing. For ONE year you've done physics on a cosmic backdrop — distance, gravity, energy, heat, waves, charge.
blocks.connection: Unit 8 opens the BUILD arc. Starting next week you're building a working machine, by hand, with what you've learned. Today is the explicit handoff: we preview the parts kit, you predict the numbers, and Unit 8 Day 1 you'll measure to compare.

blocks.markdown:
## ★ Car Project — parts preview

Every part in the kit traces back to a unit of this year. Theory becomes engineering:

| Part | Traces to | The physics |
|---|---|---|
| 9V battery | Unit 7 Day 4 | voltage = energy per charge (V = U/q) |
| Wires + switch | Unit 7 Days 5-6 | current (I = q/t) + resistance (V = IR) |
| DC motor | Unit 7 Day 15 | current-in-field → torque (F = I·L·B) |
| Gears (reduction) | Unit 2 | mechanical advantage (input/output gear teeth) |
| Wheels | Unit 2 | rotational → translational motion (v = ωr) |
| Starting impulse | Unit 3 | Δp = F·Δt — what gets the car moving |
| Top-speed kinetic energy | Unit 4 | KE = ½mv² — what the motor builds up |
| Friction (wheels, bearings) | Unit 2 + Unit 5 | μ·N + heat dissipation |

The electrical heart of the build is the simplest circuit in this unit: **battery → switch → motor → back**. One series loop. Today you predict its numbers; next week you measure them.

blocks.title: The Car Project circuit — one series loop
blocks.caption: Battery → switch → motor → back. This is the exact circuit you will build on Day 1 of Unit 8. Everything you verified in the Day 10 lab applies to it directly.

blocks.instruction: Draw the Car Project with each part labeled with its origin unit. The visual centerpiece of this page — and of the bridge from Unit 7 to Unit 8.
prompts: Battery, switch, motor, gears, wheels — all drawn and labeled.
Next to each part: the unit it traces to (use the parts table).
Circle the Unit 7 parts — that's what this week was for.

blocks.prompt: ★ STEP 1 — PREDICT MOTOR CURRENT. Given: Car Project uses a 9V battery. Estimated motor resistance: R ≈ 3-5 Ω (we'll measure on Day 1 of Unit 8). Predict the current the motor will draw.
blocks.work:
If R = 3 Ω: I = 9/3 = 3 A
If R = 4 Ω: I = 9/4 = 2.25 A
If R = 5 Ω: I = 9/5 = 1.8 A
→ Expected range: I ≈ 1.8 to 3.0 A
blocks.answer: PREDICTION: the motor will draw between 1.8 A and 3.0 A. Most likely ≈ 2-2.5 A.

blocks.prompt: ★ STEP 2 — PREDICT MOTOR POWER. Using your predicted current from Step 1, compute the electrical power delivered to the motor. (P = V·I.)
blocks.given: V = 9 V · I ≈ 1.8 to 3.0 A (your prediction)
blocks.work:
If I = 1.8 A: P = 9 · 1.8 = 16.2 W
If I = 2.25 A: P = 9 · 2.25 = 20.25 W
If I = 3.0 A: P = 9 · 3.0 = 27 W
→ Expected range: P ≈ 16 to 27 W
→ About 40% becomes motion; 60% becomes heat (Day 15).
blocks.answer: PREDICTION: motor draws ≈ 16-27 W. Mechanical power out ≈ 7-11 W.

blocks.prompt: TEAM PREDICTION — use the most likely numbers from Steps 1 and 2. Fill in the frame, then circle one: the motor will (get warm / not get warm).
blocks.frame: Our team predicts the motor will draw ___ A and consume ___ W. About ___ W will become motion; ___ W will become heat. The motor will (get warm / not get warm).

blocks.title: ★ Prediction now, measurement next week
blocks.markdown: The PREDICTION is a Marzano-3 deliverable. The MEASUREMENT happens on Day 1 of Unit 8 (next week). The TRANSFER task (Day 18) will ask you to predict for a different motor.

blocks.prompt: WHY is the Car Project's circuit wired in SERIES (battery → switch → motor → back) instead of parallel? Reason about what would happen if any one wire breaks. (Hint: a series-only circuit is what an ON/OFF switch can fully control.)
blocks.frame: A series loop has ___ path for current, so opening the switch ___. If any one wire breaks, ___.
